name: As Pontes - trail by Rio Eume and Embalsa de Ribeira
name_original: As Pontes – sendero por Río Eume y Embalsa de Ribeira
region_intro: Hiking around As Pontes De García Rodríguez offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by the ancient Atlantic forest of Fragas do Eume Natural Park and the Eume River. The region features thick forests, numerous streams, and waterfalls. A prominent feature is Lake As Pontes, Spain's largest artificial lake, created from a former open-pit mine, now surrounded by trails. Best hiking trails around As Pontes De García Rodríguez The most popular hiking route is As Pontes - trail by Rio Eume and Embalsa de Ribeira, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the Eume River and passes by the Embalsa de Ribeira. Another top favourite among local hikers is Santa Cristina Bridge – Caaveiro Tavern loop from O Fozón, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route explores the area around the historic Santa Cristina Bridge and the Caaveiro Monastery. Local hikers also love the Carbueira Viewpoint loop from Monfero, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around As Pontes De García Rodríguez is defined by ancient Atlantic forests, the Eume River, and the unique Lake As Pontes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in As Pontes De García Rodríguez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore As Pontes De García Rodríguez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails can I find around As Pontes De García Rodríguez?
A: As Pontes De García Rodríguez offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 16 easy, 16 moderate, and 6 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in the area?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for families. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31688311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina Bridge – Caaveiro Tavern loop from O Fozón&lt;/a&gt;. This 4.3-mile (6.9 km) path explores the historic Santa Cristina Bridge and the area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint John of Caaveiro&lt;/a&gt;, offering a gentle introduction to the region's beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in As Pontes De García Rodríguez?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the region are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10502197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbueira Viewpoint loop from Monfero&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.4-mile (8.7 km) circular trail that takes you through varied terrain, offering scenic views.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful 'fervenzas' (waterfalls). You can explore routes that lead to natural wonders like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3566939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza de Naraío&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6271785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Río Xestosa&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7476337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza de Bermui&lt;/a&gt;, especially within or near the Fragas do Eume Natural Park.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while hiking?
A: Hiking in As Pontes De García Rodríguez offers a wealth of natural attractions. You'll encounter the ancient Atlantic forest of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7546440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural das Fragas do Eume&lt;/a&gt;, the picturesque Eume River, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; (part of Lake As Pontes). Many trails also lead to scenic viewpoints and through thick forests.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical and cultural sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint John of Caaveiro&lt;/a&gt;, nestled within Fragas do Eume, is a key historical site accessible via hiking paths. You can also find the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5711118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and old mills, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in As Pontes De García Rodríguez?
A: The trails in As Pontes De García Rodríguez are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's diverse natural landscapes, the tranquility of the ancient forests, and the unique experience of walking around Lake As Pontes, a remarkable environmental rehabilitation project.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991239595/as-pontes-trail-by-rio-eume-and-embalsa-de-ribeira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Pontes - trail by Rio Eume and Embalsa de Ribeira&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 13.5-mile (21.7 km) trail that follows the Eume River and passes by the Embalsa de Ribeira, typically taking over 6 hours to complete.

Q: Is it possible to access hiking trails using public transport?
A: While many trailheads are best reached by car, some areas, particularly around the Fragas do Eume Natural Park, may have limited public transport options or shuttle services during peak seasons. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or park information for specific access points before planning your trip.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in As Pontes De García Rodríguez?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions. During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the natural landscapes, especially the Atlantic forests, are vibrant with color. Summer can be warm, while winter offers a quieter experience, though some paths might be muddy.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Yes, the varied terrain of As Pontes De García Rodríguez includes several routes with rewarding viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10502197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbueira Viewpoint loop from Monfero&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is specifically named for its scenic vistas. Additionally, the 'Columpio de As Pontes' near Lake As Pontes offers excellent panoramic views of the lake and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in As Pontes De García Rodríguez, especially in natural park areas like Fragas do Eume. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check specific park regulations beforehand. Always clean up after your pet.
tips: Hydroelectric dam on the Eume river creating the Ribeira reservoir. Difficult circular hike around this nature reserve
If it rains you will find mud but it is worth the walk
